program_en: B.Sc. in Exercise Science and Health Education
program_chi: 運動科學與健康教育理學士
learning_outcomes:
ESHEN_Learning Outcomes_ENG 1.  |  Major Programme:
Graduates of the B.Sc. Programme in
Exercise Science and Health Education (ESHE) will be able to:
(1)  |  demonstrate specialist knowledge and
professional skills in Exercise Science and Health Education; (K/S)
(2)  |  plan and implement a programme of
investigative work; (K/S/V)
(3)  |  demonstrate an informed, critical, and
innovative approach to learning through understanding and applying knowledge
and skills learned from the field of exercise science towards the advancement
of human health, performance and quality of life, through effective
education, promotion, evaluation, research and development; (K/S/V)
(4)  |  synthesize and evaluate strategies to enhance
professionalism in administrative and managerial roles in sports, exercise
science and health sectors; (K/S/V)
(5)  |  gather and use information as a strategic
resource to contribute creatively to the development of Exercise Science and
Health Education; (K/S/V)
(6)  |  display a critical and creative attitude in
their professional life; (K/S/V) and
(7)  |  apply their knowledge and skills in exercise
science and health practices. (K/S)
K = Knowledge
outcomes S = Skills outcomes V = Values and attitude outcomes
study_scheme:
Exercise Science and Health Education Exercise Science and Health Education Applicable to students admitted in 2024-25
Major Programme Requirement (for Associate Degree or Higher Diploma holders admitted to
senior-year places)
Students are required to complete a
minimum of 55 units of
courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Required Courses:
(a)  |  Core Courses in Exercise Science and
Health  |  38
PHPC1001, SPED2520, 2540, 2600, 2610,
2710, 2720, 2810, 3430, 3820, 3910,
3920, 4570
(b)  |  Core Skills Courses  |  3
SPED2010, 2110, 2141
(c)  |  Research Experience  |  6
SPED3630[a], 4900[a]
(d)  |  Internship  |  2
SPED4201[a]
2.  |  Elective Courses:
(a)  |  Theoretical
Courses in Exercise Science One course chosen
from the following:  |  3
SPED3550, 3731, 4560, 4640, 4830
(b)  |  Theoretical Courses in Health  |  3
One course chosen
from the following:
CHPR3002#, 4012#, PHPC2007#, 2009#
Total:  |  55
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  SPED courses at 2000 and above level as included in the Major
Programme Requirement as well as those labeled as # will be included in
the calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  Students who have completed 3 units of Core Skills Courses will
be eligible to apply for exemption of 1 unit of University Core Physical Education
Requirement. Students are required to apply for the exemption. When
exemption from a particular course is recognized, students can only be
exempted from the course but not the units.
[a]  |  Capstone courses
